sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच।
संन्यासः कर्मयोगश्च निःश्रेयसकरावुभौ।
तयोस्तु कर्मसंन्यासात्कर्मयोगो विशिष्यते॥
transliteration:
śrī-bhagavān uvāca
sannyāsaḥ karma-yogaś ca
niḥśreyasa-karāv ubhau
tayos tu karma-sannyāsāt
karma-yogo viśiṣyate
literal_meaning: The Lord said: Both renunciation of action and action in yoga lead to the highest good; yet, of the two, action in yoga is superior to renunciation of action.
contextual_meaning: Krishna reassures Arjuna that two valid psychological coping strategies—total withdrawal (sannyāsa) and engaged mindfulness (karma-yoga)—both reduce existential anxiety, but the latter keeps the nervous system regulated while staying socially connected, making it the safer default.
psychological_state: Decision-paralysis between total disengagement and responsible engagement; needs permission to act without guilt.
trigger_scenario: A burned-out healthcare worker torn between quitting the profession (to escape moral injury) and staying to serve patients with mindful boundaries.
anchor_text: Stay engaged with life while keeping your inner peace intact.
